turn1_prompt: From a bag containing 6 distinct types of fruit, you randomly select 4 fruits with replacement (i.e., after each selection, the fruit is returned to the bag). How many different unordered sets of 4 fruits can you obtain? (Two selections are considered the same if they have the same multiset of fruit types, regardless of the order in which they were drawn.)
mechanism_primary: The default convention is that 'unordered sets' imply combinations without repetition (C(n,k)), but with replacement allowed, the correct count is for combinations with repetition (multisets), using C(n+k-1,k).
audit_notes: Sprint v2 Phase 4. Canary: 2/2. Frontier (anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6): correct. Adversary scores: tef=1, cr=2
